segments.title: Hawaii protectorate requires visible naval presence
segments.summary: The scenario turns an 1824 royal promise into a British protectorate sustained by ships, a naval hospital, coaling, and Pacific trade links.
segments.body: Britain does not annex Hawaii as a colony, but a royal promise creates a protectorate that needs physical naval infrastructure and makes outside interference harder.

evidence.note: The transcript frames the viable point of divergence as George IV accepting a deathbed request and creating a protectorate rather than a colony.
The transcript explains the resulting British ships, naval hospital, coaling role, merchants, and deterrent effect on outside interference.
